violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. -Employee observed handling raw pork and then serving food to customers with no handwash. -No handwashing observed during inspection. Glove changes without handwashing.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. -Open in-use bag of rice sitting on floor in back prep area.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. -Raw chicken sitting above ready to eat food and above squid in walk in cooler.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution between uses. -Multiple wet cloths sitting on food prep tables.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Scoop for rice sitting in container of water. Keep scoop above 140, below 41 or change out every 4 hrs. -Scoops for ice bin and spices sitting with handles in product.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Right prep cooler on cook line at 46-47F.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. -Pork slices sitting in pan of water in prep sink. Needs to be thawed under cold running water or under refrigeration.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Shelving edges above microwave need to be painted or sealed. -Cardboard is not a cleanable surface and cannot be used on shelving.

Food-contact surfaces - Design and construction. — --Multiuse food-contact surfaces did not meet design and/or construction requirements. -All scoops need to have handles. -Single use cup was being used to scoop rice.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. -2 gas burners in use not under hood.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean inside microwave, inside all coolers, shelving in walk in cooler, food storage tubs in walk in cooler, all prep tables.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean all storage shelving, fan covers in walk in cooler, bus/utility cart and cloth curtain going in/out of kitchen.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Hood and filters need cleaned.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. -Clean dishes sitting on rack in dirty containers. -Knife used on cook line stored between cooler and shelf
Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not kept clean or maintained. -Handwash sink was dirty.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room was not provided with sufficient mechanical ventilation. -Fan not working in men's room.

Receptacles for refuse, recyclables, and returnables. — --Equipment used for refuse, recyclables and returnables was not clean or not cleaned at a frequency to prevent buildup of soil. -Clean or replace garbage cans in kitchen.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc.) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -Floors, walls and ceilings need deep cleaned as they are very dirty.
General premises. — --Door used for ventilation purposes was not properly protected against the entry of insects and rodents. -Back door to kitchen was propped open.
Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. -Dirty wet mop sitting in mop sink.
Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. -Spray bottle in kitchen not labeled.
Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. -Items in walk in freezer stored below heavily iced up condenser.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution between uses. -2 wet cloths sitting on prep table.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. -Scoops for front ice bay and in spice bags have handle in product. Store with handle out of product.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. -Squid, fish and chicken stored in sink. Needs to be thawed under cold running water or under refrigeration.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Wood storage shelves in dry storage are bare wood and need to be painted.

Food-contact surfaces - Design and construction. — --Multiuse food-contact surfaces did not meet design and/or construction requirements. -Scoops used for spices need to have handles, Was using single use bowls.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not provided over all cooking equipment. -Single burner stove not under hood system.

General equipment and utensils - Maintenance and operation. — --Nonfood contact equipment/utensil was not maintained in a good repair or sound condition. -Walk in freezer is heavily iced up and needs cleaned up and defrosted.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Front microwave needs cleaned. -Cutting boards need cleaned. -Clean inside all coolers on cook line.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Clean shelving in walk in cooler, back prep area, and in front serving area. -Clean fans in walk in cooler.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Filters need cleaned.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Clean inside and out of dishwasher.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room used by women did not contain at least one covered waste receptacle.
Receptacles for refuse, recyclables, and returnables. — --Clean all garbage cans in backroom.
General premises. — --Perimeter walls and roof did not effectively protect establishment from the weather and entry of insects, rodents, and other animals. -Back entry door does not close tight.
